title: Workflow Definition
description: Data-driven state machine for multi-agent workflows. Each workflow is a directed graph of states with role assignments, prompt templates, tool permissions, and transition rules. The LLM gets one prompt for one state and a gated tool set. Transitions are deterministic code, not LLM decisions.

id.description: Unique workflow identifier (e.g., 'dev-qa-merge'). Must be lowercase with hyphens.

description.description: What this workflow does. Shown in generated documentation.

initialState.description: State name to assign when a new task enters this workflow. Must reference a key in 'states'.

terminalStates.description: State names that signify the task is complete (no further transitions). Each must reference a key in 'states'.

globalContext.description: Context variables available to every prompt template via {{variable}} substitution. Merged with per-task context; task context wins on conflict.

states.description: State definitions keyed by state name (uppercase by convention, e.g., IMPLEMENTING). Must include at least the initialState and one terminal state.

StateDefinition.description: A single state in the workflow graph.

role.description: Which agent role handles this state. The engine resolves roles to agent hostnames via the team roster.

description.description: What this state does. Used in logging, debugging, and generated state diagrams.

prompt.description:
Prompt template sent to the LLM when executing this state. This is the 'how' -- procedural instructions for the agent. The 'what' (task-specific details) comes from the assignment's taskPrompt field.

Uses {{variable}} substitution. Available variables include globalContext keys, task context accumulated from prior states (branch, commitSha, testResults, etc.), and any requiredOutputs produced by earlier states.

Terminal states may have an empty prompt.

allowedTools.description:
Recommended tools for this state (ADVISORY, not a whitelist). These are rendered into the prompt as guidance but all SDK built-in tools remain available. This avoids catastrophic failures from overly restrictive tool gating.

Accepts individual tool names or group names.

Tool groups:
  - mailbox:read  -> check_mailbox, read_message, archive_message
  - mailbox:send  -> send_message, send_broadcast
  - reporting     -> send_completion_report, escalate_issue
  - team          -> get_team_roster, find_agents_by_role, find_agents_by_capability, get_agent_info
  - terminal      -> run_in_terminal, get_terminal_output
  - file_ops      -> read_file, create_file, replace_string_in_file
  - git           -> run_in_terminal

restrictedTools.description:
Tools explicitly blocked in this state (ENFORCED deny-list). Unlike allowedTools (advisory), these are actually enforced -- the engine will refuse calls to tools on this list. Use sparingly for safety boundaries.

Examples: prevent QA from 'git push', prevent developer from 'send_broadcast'.

Accepts individual tool names or group names (same as allowedTools).

requiredOutputs.description:
Context keys that MUST be present in StateExecutionResult.outputs before the engine allows a success transition. If any are missing, the transition is treated as a failure.

Examples: ['branch', 'commitSha'], ['testResults', 'verdict']

maxRetries.description: Maximum consecutive failures before escalation. When retryCount exceeds this, the engine follows the onFailure transition.

timeoutMs.description: Hard time limit for this state in milliseconds. If exceeded, treated as a failure.

onEntryCommands.description: Shell commands executed directly by the engine (child_process.execSync) on entering this state, BEFORE the main work items. The LLM is not involved. Template variables ({{branch}}, {{taskId}}, {{role}}, {{state}}) are resolved from accumulated task context + globalContext.

onExitCommands.description: Shell commands executed directly by the engine on exiting this state, AFTER the main work items complete but BEFORE the state transition. Typically used to ensure git add/commit/push so downstream agents can see the work.

entryActions.description: Actions the engine executes automatically on entering this state. These are code actions, not LLM actions.

exitActions.description: Actions the engine executes automatically on successful exit from this state.

tasks.description: Required tasks that the decomposition MUST include as work items. Each string describes a required deliverable or activity. The decomposition prompt builder composes these into: 'Work items MUST include: <task1>, <task2>, ...'. Gives workflow authors structured control over what the LLM includes in its breakdown.

decompositionPrompt.description: Additional free-form guidance injected into the decomposition prompt. Use for domain-specific advice that doesn't fit the structured 'tasks' array (e.g. ordering constraints, grouping rules). When both 'tasks' and 'decompositionPrompt' are present, the tasks sentence is rendered first.

exitEvaluation.description: Structured exit evaluation for this state. When present, the engine asks the LLM a constrained question after work items complete and BEFORE the transition fires. The parsed answer determines success/failure routing. When absent, the engine falls back to regex-based fail detection or default success.

permissions.description: SDK permission overrides for this state. Applied by the agent on state entry (after onEntryCommands) and cleared on state exit (after onExitCommands). Use to enforce read-only validation states (e.g. { "write": "deny" }) at the SDK permission layer — the LLM physically cannot perform blocked operations. Only specified keys are overridden; unspecified kinds keep their base config policy.

write.description: Override for file write permission. Set to 'deny' to block all file creation/modification during this state.

mcp.description: Override for MCP server tool call permission.

Transitions.description: Deterministic transition rules. These are NOT LLM decisions -- the engine evaluates StateExecutionResult.success and follows the appropriate edge.

onSuccess.description: State to move to on success. Must reference a key in 'states', or null for terminal states.

onFailure.description: State to move to on failure. If same as the current state, the task retries in place (up to maxRetries). If different, moves to that state (typically a REWORK state or ESCALATED). Null means terminal.

StateCommand.description: A deterministic shell command executed directly by the engine (child_process.execSync) at state entry or exit. The engine logs stdout/stderr and respects failOnError. Template variables ({{branch}}, {{taskId}}, {{role}}, {{state}}) are resolved before execution.

command.description: Shell command to execute. Supports {{variable}} template substitution from task context and globalContext. Examples: 'git fetch origin', 'git push origin {{branch}}', 'git checkout {{branch}}'.

reason.description: Human-readable reason logged by the engine for diagnostics. Provides context for why the command exists (e.g. 'If origin/{{branch}} does not exist yet, skip this step').

failOnError.description: If true (default), a command failure aborts subsequent commands in the sequence. Set to false for best-effort commands that may legitimately fail (e.g. resetting to a branch that does not exist on a fresh repo).

captureAs.description: When set, the trimmed stdout is stored as a workflow context variable under this key. Designed for deterministic commands like 'git rev-parse HEAD' whose output must be reliably captured (e.g. as 'commitSha'). The captured value is merged into StateExecutionResult.outputs before the transition fires.

StateAction.description: Automated action executed by the engine (not the LLM) on state entry or exit.

type.description:
Action type:
  - send_to_role: auto-send the task message to the state's role
  - set_context: set a context variable
  - log: emit a structured log entry

params.description: Action-specific parameters. For send_to_role: no params needed. For set_context: {key: value}. For log: {message: '...', level: 'info'}.

ExitEvaluation.description: Structured exit evaluation that replaces regex-based fail detection. After work items complete, the engine sends a constrained question to the LLM. The response is parsed deterministically and mapped to a success or failure outcome, which drives the state transition.

prompt.description: Question asked to the LLM after work items complete. Should be answerable with a single word (true/false for boolean, or one of the declared choices for enum). Supports {{variable}} template substitution from task context and globalContext.

responseFormat.description:
Expected response format.
  - boolean: LLM must answer true or false (yes/no also accepted)
  - enum: LLM must answer with one of the declared choices

choices.description: For enum format: the valid choices the LLM can pick from. Ignored for boolean format.

mapping.description:
Maps parsed response values to routing outcomes.

For boolean: keys are 'true' and 'false'.
For enum: keys are each of the declared choices.
Values are 'success' or 'failure'.

defaultOutcome.description: Outcome when the LLM response cannot be parsed into any recognized value. Defaults to 'failure' (safe default -- forces rework rather than silently passing).
